title: Can My Patient Fly After A Treatment？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Can My Patient Fly After A Treatment？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: Here's another common question my client wants to fly away after having a treatment i presume it's on a plane [Music] so what's the real dynamic going on here when patients are asking about flying is there a risk with changes in air pressure after having a cosmetic injectable tre…

segments.text: here's another common question my client wants to fly away after having a treatment i presume it's on a plane [Music] so what's the real dynamic going on here when patients are asking about flying is there a risk with changes in air pressure after having a cosmetic injectable treatment no there is no risk at all some of this does come from you know if you have cosmetic surgery particularly a breast enhancement you can't fly afterwards because if any chest any air is got into the chest cavity and expands you might cause problems with breathing that's the theory anyway so i think that kind of infuses where what we think about in terms of injectables but really there's no risk in terms of being in a plane what the risk is about being away from your practitioner if something bad happens and for that reason if you're doing anything to do with dermal fillers i recommend a minimum of 48 hours because that gives you the chance to diagnose and treat a vascular occlusion and other things that might happen when you're on the plane can wait

and if you're developing an infection possibly even a reaction that can wait until you land you'll be able to find a doctor they'll probably be able to help you can probably support your client from afar in those sorts of circumstances but vascular occlusion is very different there's a limited period of time you need to act quickly and so we say a 48-hour gap between when you have your treatment and when you should be prepared to actually get on a plane where you can't get medical assistance so that's how i frame it toxin is very different i don't think there's anything with botox and that you need to worry about and you could fly straight away i don't see that there's any particular risk other than maybe an incredibly tiny chance of anaphylactic reaction but you're going to know that within an hour so as long as you're not treating them in the airport terminal i don't think there's any risk there at all so i'd leave 48 hours you
